_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Surgery for Haematoma - Intracranial Child - subdural
clinical_indications: Subdural hematoma (SDH) in children due to hemorrhage into the potential space between the dura and the arachnoid membranes, often due to abusive head injury, requiring surgical intervention.

clinical_thresholds.note: warrants emergent consultation and evaluation by a neurosurgeon

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, and planned line of management

claim.label: Glasgow coma score (GCS)/ Pediatric GCS documentation
In case of accident FIR report (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, and planned line of management?
Did clinical evaluation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?
Was clinical presentation, GCS/Pediatric GCS score and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the CT/MRI Brain report indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
In case of accidental injury was FIR done?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or lacking details on history, symptoms, indication, or planned management.
Pediatric GCS score not documented, or not indicative of surgery (e.g., above 12 without justification).
CT/MRI Brain report not indicative of surgery, or imaging not confirming diagnosis.
FIR report not available for cases of accidental injury (if applicable).
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or without daily vitals and treatment details.
Detailed Procedure/Operative Notes missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary missing or without follow-up advice.

clinical_key_pointers: Subdural hematoma (SDH) forms when there is hemorrhage into the potential space between the dura and the arachnoid membranes.
SDH in children differs significantly from SDH in adults because abusive head injury is a common etiology, especially in pediatric patients <2 years of age.
Any pediatric trauma patient with a Glasgow Coma Score (GCS) ≤12 warrants emergent consultation and evaluation by a neurosurgeon.
Children with a GCS ≤8 should undergo intracranial ICP monitoring.
surgical_options: Chronic Subdural: Subdural peritoneal shunt placement
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Manage patient according to principles of advanced trauma life support. Immobilize cervical spine. Treat hypoxemia. Assess airway, breathing, circulation, and disability and initiate supportive care. Endotracheally intubate children with GCS ≤8 or rapidly worsening mental status. If impending herniation, provide hyperosmolar therapy (3 percent saline or mannitol, refer to UpToDate topics on the management of increased ICP in children). Ensure definitive management by a neurosurgeon with pediatric expertise.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Any pediatric trauma patient with a Glasgow Coma Score (GCS) ≤12 warrants emergent consultation and evaluation by a neurosurgeon.
